name: FalkorDB QueryWeaver MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from FalkorDB (FalkorDB org) for QueryWeaver — a graph query assistant for FalkorDB, the ultra-fast Redis-compatible graph database. Enables AI agents to run Cypher queries, explore graph schemas, and analyze connected data in FalkorDB knowledge graphs.
use_cases: Running Cypher graph queries on FalkorDB from AI data analysis agents
Exploring graph schemas and entity relationships from knowledge graph agents
Building AI applications using FalkorDB's low-latency graph capabilities
Querying knowledge graphs for entity resolution and relationship analysis via agents
not_for: Teams not using FalkorDB (Neo4j, Amazon Neptune, or other graph databases need different MCPs)
Relational SQL databases (FalkorDB is a graph database)
Non-graph data patterns without relationship-heavy queries
best_when: A developer using FalkorDB as a knowledge graph or graph database wants AI agents to query entity relationships, traverse graphs, and analyze connected data using Cypher.
avoid_when: You use Neo4j, Amazon Neptune, or non-graph databases. Graph databases excel for relationship-heavy data; use SQL databases for tabular data.

auth.notes: FalkorDB connection with host/port and optional password (Redis AUTH). FalkorDB Cloud uses API credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: FalkorDB open source is free to self-host; FalkorDB Cloud has free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: FalkorDB Cloud paid plans for managed deployment
pricing.notes: FalkorDB is open source (RSALv2). MCP server is free open source from FalkorDB org.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by FalkorDB instance performance — extremely fast graph engine

known_agent_gotchas: Cypher query syntax — agents must know Cypher (similar to Neo4j but FalkorDB-specific extensions)
FalkorDB is Redis-compatible — can use Redis auth if password set
Graph schema exploration needed before querying — agents should inspect schema first
Official from FalkorDB org — actively maintained and supported
agent_readiness.security_notes: Official FalkorDB. Graph data may be proprietary knowledge. Redis AUTH for access control. TLS recommended for remote deployments.

security_score_components.security_notes: Official. Graph data may be sensitive. Redis AUTH. TLS for remote.
